key_findings: High prenatal lead exposure (10-254 µg/dL) is associated with lower cognitive scores at 2 years.

summaries.title: Selenium protects neonates against neurotoxicity from prenatal exposure to manganese.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used umbilical cord serum measurements and Neonatal Behavioral Neurological Assessment (NBNA) tests conducted during recruitment, rather than an existing EHR database.
summaries.summary: An epidemiological study investigating whether Selenium (Se) provides a protective effect against neurotoxicity caused by prenatal Manganese (Mn) exposure in neonates.
key_findings: Mn was negatively associated with NBNA scores at low Se levels
High Se levels reduced the adverse effects of Mn on NBNA scores
Mn/Se ratio was moderately correlated with NBNA scores
summaries.population: 933 mother-newborn pairs in Shanghai, China

summaries.title: Prenatal exposure to organophosphate pesticides and neurobehavioral development of neonates: a birth cohort study in Shenyang, China.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None. Data were collected via questionnaires and urine analysis.
summaries.summary: A birth cohort study investigating the association between prenatal organophosphate pesticide (OP) exposure and neonatal neurobehavioral development in Shenyang, China.
key_findings: Prenatal OP exposure was significantly associated with neonatal neurobehavioral development.
Prenatal total DAP concentrations were inversely associated with NBNA scores.
A 10-fold increase in DAP concentrations was associated with a 1.78 decrease in Summary NBNA scores.
captured_features: Urinary DAP metabolites (DMP, DMTP, DEP, DETP)
summaries.population: 249 pregnant women and their neonates in Shenyang, China

summaries.title: Long-term effects of maternal deprivation on cholinergic system in rat brain.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses Wistar rats as an animal model; no human electronic health records were used.
summaries.summary: The study examines the long-term effects of maternal deprivation (MD) on the cholinergic system in rat brains, specifically measuring acetylcholinesterase (AChE) activity and cholinergic fiber density in the hippocampus and retrosplenial (RS) cortex.
key_findings: Significant decrease of AChE activity in the cortex of MD rats
Significant increase of AChE activity in the hippocampus of MD rats
Significant increase in cholinergic fiber density in the CA1 region of the hippocampus
Significant decrease in cholinergic fiber density in the RS cortex
summaries.population: Male Wistar rats

summaries.title: Determining prenatal, early childhood and cumulative long-term lead exposure using micro-spatial deciduous dentine levels.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study uses biological samples (blood, bone, and teeth) from a prospective cohort.
summaries.summary: The study validates micro-spatial dentine lead (Pb) levels as a biomarker to reconstruct the timing and intensity of prenatal, early childhood, and cumulative long-term lead exposure.
key_findings: Dentine Pb formed at birth was significantly associated with cord blood Pb (r = 0.69).
Prenatal dentine Pb associated with maternal patella Pb (r = 0.48) and tibia Pb (r = 0.35).
Dentine Pb formed at 3 months was significantly associated with childhood blood Pb (r = 0.64).
Secondary dentine correlated positively with the cumulative blood lead index (r = 0.38).
captured_features: Childhood serial blood Pb (3 months to 6 years)
summaries.population: 85 subjects in a prospective pregnancy cohort (mothers and children up to 6 years)

summaries.title: Prenatal exposure to maternal bereavement and childbirths in the offspring: a population-based cohort study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Data is stored at the secure platform of Denmark Statistics, which is the central authority on Danish statistics.
summaries.summary: A population-based cohort study investigating the association between prenatal exposure to maternal bereavement (stress) and the fertility (age at first child and number of childbirths) of offspring in Denmark and Sweden.
key_findings: No overall association between prenatal exposure to maternal stress and having a child in early adulthood.
Females born to mothers who lost a parent had a slightly reduced probability of having children (HR 0.97).
Females born to mothers who lost a spouse (HR 1.29), sibling (HR 1.13), or another child (HR 1.09) had an increased probability of having children.
captured_features: Maternal bereavement (timing, type of relative deceased)
summaries.population: Subjects born in Denmark after 1968 and Sweden after 1973
limitations: Longer time of follow-up is necessary in order to reach a firmer conclusion

summaries.title: Quantity and diversity of environmental microbial exposure and development of asthma: a birth cohort study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses a birth cohort with questionnaires and dust samples, not electronic health records.
summaries.summary: A prospective birth cohort study investigating the association between environmental microbial exposure (quantity and diversity) and the development of asthma, wheezing, and atopic dermatitis in children up to 6 years of age.
key_findings: A score for the total quantity of microbial exposure (ergosterol, muramic acid, and endotoxin) was significantly associated with asthma incidence in an inverted-U shape.
The microbial diversity score was inversely associated with the risk of wheezing.
Microbial diversity score was significantly associated with sensitization to inhalant allergens in an inverted-U shape.
summaries.population: 410 children born in Eastern and Middle Finland

summaries.title: Altered cortical thickness following prenatal sodium valproate exposure.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Participants were recruited to a prospective long-term cognitive outcomes study and via hospital community advertisements.
summaries.summary: A study investigating cortical thickness in children with prenatal sodium valproate (VPA) exposure compared to age- and sex-matched controls.
key_findings: Increased left inferior frontal gyrus (IFG) and left pericalcarine sulcus thickness in VPA-exposed children
Association between VPA dose and right IFG thickness
Absence of normal asymmetry in the IFG region of VPA-exposed children
summaries.population: 16 children (mean age 7.2) with prenatal VPA exposure; 16 controls

summaries.title: Prenatal organochlorine and methylmercury exposure and memory and learning in school-age children in communities near the New Bedford Harbor Superfund site, Massachusetts.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: Assessment of the association between low-level prenatal exposure to PCBs, organochlorines, and methylmercury with memory and learning in school-age children near the New Bedford Harbor Superfund Site.
summaries.population: School-age children in communities near the New Bedford Harbor Superfund Site, Massachusetts

summaries.title: Prenatal exposure to arsenic and cadmium impacts infectious disease-related genes within the glucocorticoid receptor signal transduction pathway.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None mentioned in the provided text.
summaries.summary: The text is a collection of research titles and snippets exploring the impacts of prenatal arsenic and cadmium exposure on the human epigenome, immune system, and glucocorticoid receptor signaling.
key_findings: Prenatal arsenic exposure is associated with impaired thymic function and altered DNA methylation.
Cadmium exposure dysregulates signaling pathways in the thymus.
Arsenic exposure in pregnancy increases risks of infant infections.
summaries.population: Newborns, infants, pregnant women, and adults
limitations: The text is a list of titles/snippets, not a single cohesive study.

summaries.title: Prenatal exposure to endocrine disrupting chemicals in relation to thyroid hormone levels in infants - a Dutch prospective cohort study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: T4 levels in heel prick blood spots were obtained through the neonatal screening programme in the Netherlands.
summaries.summary: Prospective cohort study assessing the association between prenatal exposure to endocrine disrupting chemicals (EDCs) and thyroxine (T4) levels in newborns.
key_findings: Girls in the highest quartile of DDE and PFOA exposure showed increased T4 levels.
Boys showed lower T4 levels in the second quartile of PFOS and PFOA exposure, which were attenuated after adjustment.
summaries.population: newborns in a Dutch mother-child cohort

summaries.title: Perinatal Exposure to Traffic-Related Air Pollution and Atopy at 1 Year of Age in a Multi-Center Canadian Birth Cohort Study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study is a birth cohort study involving participating families, not an EHR-based study.
summaries.summary: A multi-center Canadian birth cohort study investigating the association between perinatal exposure to traffic-related air pollution (TRAP) and atopy in children at 1 year of age.
key_findings: The study investigates the role of TRAP in allergic sensitization, though specific results are not fully detailed in the provided text.
summaries.population: Canadian infants

summaries.title: Association between levels of persistent organic pollutants in adipose tissue and cryptorchidism in early childhood: a case-control study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used subcutaneous adipose tissue biopsies taken during surgery, not EHR data.
summaries.summary: A case-control study investigating the association between prenatal exposure to persistent organic pollutants (PCBs, PCDD/Fs, and PBDEs) in adipose tissue and congenital cryptorchidism in children.
key_findings: Total-TEq (dioxins and dioxin-like PCBs) and sum of PCDD/Fs were positively associated with cryptorchidism (OR 3.21 and 3.69, respectively).
Sum of PCBs was close to significant (OR 1.92).
Sum of PBDEs was not significantly associated (OR 0.86).
summaries.population: Children in Finland and Denmark (44 cases, 38 controls)

summaries.title: Obesogen Holdover: Prenatal Exposure Predicts Cardiometabolic Risk Factors in Childhood.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study used the Rhea cohort with direct blood samples and physical measurements.
summaries.summary: A study investigating the association between prenatal exposure to persistent organic pollutants (POPs) and cardiometabolic risk factors in children at age 4.
key_findings: A 10-fold increase in maternal HCB concentrations was associated with higher risks of generalized and abdominal obesity, greater skinfold thickness, higher systolic blood pressure, and a modest increase in child BMI z-score.
Prenatal DDE exposure was associated with higher BMI z-score, increased risk of abdominal obesity, and higher diastolic blood pressure.
PCBs were not associated with any of the assessed risk factors.
captured_features: Maternal blood concentrations of DDE, HCB, and PCBs
Child body measurements (weight, height, waist circumference, skinfold thickness)
Maternal age, pre-pregnancy BMI, weight gain during pregnancy, breastfeeding duration
summaries.population: Nearly 700 mother-child pairs from the Rhea cohort in Greece
summaries.exposure_domain: Persistent organic pollutants (POPs): DDE, HCB, PCBs
limitations: Not completely clear what the findings mean for future health outcomes
Sex-specific effects were not explored in this study

summaries.title: Early-Life Exposure to Clostridium leptum Causes Pulmonary Immunosuppression.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study uses an infant mouse model.
summaries.summary: The study investigates the effects of early-life Clostridium leptum exposure on the development of asthma in infant mice, specifically looking at its role in pulmonary immunosuppression.
key_findings: Early-life C. leptum exposure increased regulatory T (Treg) cells in cesarean-delivered mice.
Mice exposed to C. leptum and ovalbumin (OVA) did not develop typical asthma phenotypes.
C. leptum exposure induced an immunosuppressive environment in the lung, inhibiting Th1, Th2, Th9, and Th17 cell responses.
captured_features: Th1, Th2, Th9, Th17 cell subsets
summaries.population: infant mice

summaries.title: Long-term effect of early-life stress from earthquake exposure on working memory in adulthood.
summaries.ehr_evidence: No EHR or linked health data mentioned; subjects were enrolled and tested using standardized cognitive assessments (BVMT-R and HVLT-R).
summaries.summary: A study investigating the long-term effects of early-life stress from the 1976 Tangshan earthquake on working memory performance in adulthood.
key_findings: Infant exposure to earthquake-related stress impairs visuospatial memory in adulthood.
Fetuses in the second and third trimesters are more vulnerable to stress-induced damage than those in the first trimester.
Education level and early-life earthquake exposure are independent risk factors for reduced visuospatial memory.
summaries.population: 907 subjects born and raised in Tangshan, China
limitations: Not specified in the provided text

summaries.title: Effectiveness of introducing point of care capillary testing and linking screening with routine appointments for increasing blood lead screening rates of young children: a before-after study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Screening rates were determined from routinely collected service data
summaries.summary: A before-after study evaluating the effectiveness of introducing point-of-care capillary testing and linking blood lead screening with routine child health programs (like immunizations) to increase screening rates in children in Broken Hill, Australia.
key_findings: Annual screening rate increased from 0.39 in 2008 to 0.75 in 2012.
Greatest increases were observed in children aged 11–23 and 48–59 months.
Linking screening with well-accepted mainstream child health programs is an effective strategy to improve participation.
summaries.population: Pre-school aged children in Broken Hill, Australia
limitations: Geographically specific to a mining town

summaries.title: Exposure to grass pollen--but not birch pollen--affects lung function in Swedish children.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Swedish birth cohort BAMSE clinical examination
summaries.summary: Study investigating the impact of pollen exposure (grass vs birch) and air pollutants on lung function in Swedish children.
key_findings: Grass pollen exposure the day before testing was associated with reduced FEV1 in 8-year-olds.
The negative effect was stronger in children sensitized to pollen allergens.
Birch pollen did not show a similar effect.
summaries.population: Swedish children (8 and 16 years old)

summaries.title: Effects of 4-nonylphenol on oxidant/antioxidant balance system inducing hepatic steatosis in male rat.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study is an experimental animal model using Sprague-Dawley rats.
summaries.summary: Experimental study investigating the effects of 4-nonylphenol (4-NP) on hepatic steatosis, oxidative stress, and gene expression in male rats.
key_findings: 4-NP induces hepatic steatosis and apoptosis in rats.
Up-regulated genes involved in apoptosis, hepatotoxicity, and oxidative stress were observed.
Increased ROS and decreased antioxidant enzymes were found in 4-NP exposed rats.
Elevated serum GOT, GPT, LDH, and gamma-GT were observed.
summaries.population: 24 male Sprague-Dawley rats

summaries.title: Mitochondrial oxidative DNA damage and exposure to particulate air pollution in mother-newborn pairs.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used the ENVIRONAGE birth cohort, which is a prospective birth cohort, not an EHR or linked health database.
summaries.summary: Study investigating the association between particulate matter (PM) exposure during pregnancy and mitochondrial oxidative DNA damage (8-OHdG) in mothers and newborns.
key_findings: PM10 exposure during entire pregnancy and last trimester associated with increased maternal 8-OHdG.
PM2.5 exposure during entire pregnancy and third trimester associated with increased maternal 8-OHdG.
PM10 exposure during first and second trimesters associated with increased cord blood 8-OHdG.
summaries.population: Mother-newborn pairs (singletons) in Genk, Belgium

summaries.title: Is There a Causal Relation between Maternal Acetaminophen Administration and ADHD?
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study uses a murine model.
summaries.summary: An experimental study using a murine model to investigate whether prenatal acetaminophen (APAP) exposure causes hyperkinetic dysfunction (ADHD-like behavior) in offspring.
key_findings: No significant differences in locomotor activity, brain volumes, or neuronal quantifications were found in offspring exposed to APAP.

summaries.title: Prenatal Exposure to Phthalates and Anogenital Distance in Male Infants from a Low-Exposed Danish Cohort (2010-2012).

summaries.summary: Prospective study investigating the association between maternal urinary phthalate metabolites during pregnancy and anogenital distance (AGD) and penile width in male offspring in a Danish cohort.
key_findings: Not provided in the snippet

summaries.title: Severe and Moderate Asthma Exacerbations in Asthmatic Children and Exposure to Ambient Air Pollutants.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Exacerbations of asthma... were deﬁned as one hospitalization or emergency room visit with a diagnosis of asthma... from a population-based cohort of asthmatic children in the province of Quebec (Canada).
summaries.summary: The study assessed the association between birth and annual exposure to outdoor air pollutants (O3, NO2, and PM2.5) and severe asthma exacerbations in a population-based cohort of asthmatic children in Quebec, Canada.
key_findings: Time-dependent exposure to NO2, O3, and PM2.5 was significantly associated with asthma exacerbations.
No associations were found between exposure at birth to NO2 or O3 and asthma exacerbations.

summaries.title: Bisphenol A and Adiposity in an Inner-City Birth Cohort.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses a prospective birth cohort (CCCEH) with direct urine samples and anthropometric measurements.
summaries.summary: A prospective birth cohort study investigating the association between prenatal and early-childhood bisphenol A (BPA) exposure and childhood adiposity measures (FMI, %BF, WC) in a New York City birth cohort.
key_findings: Prenatal urinary BPA concentrations were positively associated with child age 7 FMI, %BF, and WC.
Child urinary BPA concentrations were not associated with childhood anthropometric outcomes.

summaries.title: Moderate Prenatal Cadmium Exposure and Adverse Birth Outcomes: a Role for Sex-Specific Differences?
summaries.ehr_evidence: The sample was derived from the ALSPAC study, a population-based study investigating environmental and genetic influences on the health, behaviour, and development of children.
summaries.summary: Study investigating the association between moderate prenatal cadmium exposure and birth outcomes (birthweight, head circumference, and crown-heel length) in a large cohort of pregnant women, highlighting potential sex-specific differences.
key_findings: Maternal blood cadmium was adversely associated with birthweight and crown-heel length in the whole group.
Sex-specific analysis revealed adverse associations with birthweight, head circumference, and crown-heel length in girls, but not in boys.
summaries.population: Pregnant women in the ALSPAC cohort
limitations: Sex-specific effects may mask associations in mixed-sex studies

summaries.title: Effects of low-level prenatal lead exposure on child IQ at 4 and 8 years in a UK birth cohort study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses biological samples (whole blood) and clinical assessments from the ALSPAC birth cohort, not electronic health records.
summaries.summary: A longitudinal birth cohort study investigating the association between prenatal lead exposure and child IQ at ages 4 and 8 in a UK population.
key_findings: No association of prenatal lead exposure with child IQ at 4 or 8 years in adjusted regression models.
Positive association for IQ at age 8 in girls (verbal 0.71, performance 0.57, total 0.73).
Evidence suggesting boys may be more susceptible to prenatal lead exposure than girls.
summaries.population: Pregnant women and offspring in the ALSPAC cohort (UK)
limitations: Small sample size of offspring at 30 months (n=235)

summaries.title: Reduction of in utero lead exposures in South African populations: Positive impact of unleaded petrol.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None. Data were collected from biological fluids (maternal blood, urine, and cord blood) measured using Inductive Coupled Plasma Mass spectrometry (ICP-MS).
summaries.summary: The study assessed in utero lead (Pb) exposure and birth outcomes in a South African population following the removal of lead from petrol.
key_findings: Positive correlation between maternal blood Pb and cord blood Pb.
Geographical differences in gestational age, birth length, head circumference, Apgar score, and parity.
Positive association between cord blood Pb and head circumference in female neonates.
Maternal Pb levels correlated with race, educational status, water sources, cooking fuels, and pesticide use.
summaries.population: Pregnant women and neonates in South Africa
limitations: Study does not evaluate effects in young children or adults
Geographical differences in birth outcomes may be influenced by multiple confounding factors

summaries.title: The Hokkaido Birth Cohort Study on Environment and Children's Health: cohort profile-updated 2017.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study is a prospective birth cohort study using direct specimen collection (blood, hair, breast milk) and face-to-face tests, not EHR or linked health data.
summaries.summary: A profile and update of the Hokkaido Study on Environment and Children’s Health, a prospective birth cohort study investigating the effects of low-level environmental chemical exposures on birth outcomes and child development.
key_findings: Demonstrated effects of environmental chemical exposures on genetically susceptible populations and DNA methylation.
Identified associations between chemical exposure and birth size, neonatal hormone levels, neurobehavioral development, asthma, allergies, and infectious diseases.

summaries.title: Body Burden of Dichlorodiphenyl Dichloroethene (DDE) and Childhood Pulmonary Function.

summaries.summary: A longitudinal study in central Germany investigating the relationship between blood DDE levels and pulmonary function (FVC and FEV1) in children aged 8-10 years, specifically assessing whether the association is mediated by height and weight.
key_findings: DDE measured at eight years had significant, inverse, indirect, and total effects on FVC at ten years and FEV1 at ten years, mediated through effects of DDE on height and weight.

limitations: Need for validation in regions with ongoing, high DDT application

summaries.title: Proteomic Biomarkers for Bisphenol A-Early Exposure and Women's Thyroid Cancer.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses laboratory mice and clinical samples from 28 women thyroid cancer patients, not EHR.

ANXA6 and VCP were identified as potential proteomic biomarkers.
Positive association between blood BPA levels and mRNA expression of ANXA6 and VCP in human thyroid cancer tissues.

summaries.study_design: Experimental animal study and clinical biomarker analysis
summaries.population: Female ICR mice and 28 women thyroid cancer patients

summaries.title: Assessment of Nutritional Status of Infants Living in Arsenic-Contaminated Areas in Bangladesh and Its Association with Arsenic Exposure.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; data was collected via direct anthropometric measurements and water testing.
summaries.summary: A prospective cohort study of 120 infants in arsenic-contaminated areas of Bangladesh to assess the association between drinking water arsenic levels and nutritional status (stunting, wasting, underweight) over 9 months.
key_findings: Significant association between stunting at 9 months and household drinking water arsenic exposure ≥50 μg/L (p = 0.009).
No significant changes in other nutritional indices over time or with arsenic levels.

Tube well drinking water arsenic concentration

summaries.title: Correction to: Early-life exposure to PM2.5 and risk of acute asthma clinical encounters among children in Massachusetts: a case-crossover analysis.

summaries.summary: Correction to a study on early-life PM2.5 exposure and risk of acute asthma clinical encounters among children in Massachusetts.
key_findings: Correction of Maternal Language Preference count from 240 (0.7%) to 886 (2.7%)
summaries.population: children in Massachusetts

summaries.title: Local- and regional-scale air pollution modelling (PM(10)) and exposure assessment for pregnancy trimesters, infancy, and childhood to age 15 years: Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents And Children (ALSPAC).
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses the Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents And Children (ALSPAC) birth cohort and residential address history; no EHR or linked health data are mentioned.
summaries.summary: Development and evaluation of PM10 air pollution modelling and exposure assessment for pregnancy trimesters, infancy, and childhood (up to age 15) using the ALSPAC birth cohort.
key_findings: PM10 exposures fell from 31.2 μg/m3 at age 1 to 19.6 μg/m3 at age 15.

Reliance on dispersion modelling for historic sub-annual periods

summaries.title: Total mercury exposure in early pregnancy has no adverse association with scholastic ability of the offspring particularly if the mother eats fish.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses data from the Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents and Children (ALSPAC), which is a birth cohort study.
summaries.summary: Analysis of prenatal mercury exposure and its impact on the scholastic abilities (reading, spelling, math, science) of children in the ALSPAC cohort.
key_findings: No adverse association between prenatal mercury and scholastic abilities, provided the mother ate fish.
Math and science skills were unaffected by maternal mercury levels when fish was consumed.

summaries.title: Occupational exposure to organic solvents during pregnancy and childhood behavior: findings from the PELAGIE birth cohort (France, 2002-2013).

summaries.summary: A prospective birth cohort study investigating the longitudinal association between prenatal occupational exposure to organic solvents by mothers and the behavioral traits of their children at ages 2 and 6.
key_findings: Increased externalizing behavior at age 2 associated with prenatal solvent exposure.
Association attenuated by age 6.
No association observed for internalizing behavior.

summaries.title: Sex-Dependent Impact of Low-Level Lead Exposure during Prenatal Period on Child Psychomotor Functions.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used the Polish Mother and Child Cohort (REPRO_PL), which collected questionnaire data and biological samples.
summaries.summary: Evaluation of low-level prenatal lead exposure on child psychomotor development (up to 24 months) in a Polish cohort, focusing on sex-dependent susceptibility.
key_findings: No significant impact on girls' psychomotor abilities.
Boys showed lower cognitive scores associated with increasing cord blood lead levels (β = -2.07; p = 0.04).
No significant impact on language or motor abilities.

summaries.title: Is intrauterine exposure to acetaminophen associated with emotional and hyperactivity problems during childhood? Findings from the 2004 Pelotas birth cohort.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used data from the 2004 Pelotas birth cohort and the Strengths and Difficulties Questionnaire (SDQ).
summaries.summary: A prospective longitudinal study investigating the association between intrauterine acetaminophen exposure and emotional and hyperactivity problems in children at ages 6 and 11 in a Brazilian birth cohort.
key_findings: Acetaminophen was used by 27.5% of mothers during pregnancy.
Intrauterine exposure increased the odds of emotional problems (OR 1.47) and hyperactivity/inattention (OR 1.42) in 6-year-old boys.
No association was observed for girls at either age.
The effect size for boys decreased slightly at age 11.
summaries.population: Children in the 2004 Pelotas birth cohort (Brazil)

summaries.title: Quantitative characterization of the urine and serum metabolomes of children is essential for 'omics' studies.

summaries.title: Mitotically heritable effects of BMAA on striatal neural stem cell proliferation and differentiation.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: An in vitro study investigating the effects of the cyanobacterial toxin BMAA on the proliferation, differentiation, and mitotic heritability of neural stem cells (NSCs).
key_findings: BMAA exposure at 250 µM reduced NSC proliferation through apoptosis and G2/M arrest.
BMAA exposure at 50–100 µM reduced the differentiation of NSCs into astrocytes, oligodendrocytes, and neurons.
BMAA-induced changes in neurons derived from treated NSCs were mitotically heritable to daughter cells.
BMAA-induced morphological alterations included reduced neurite length and decreased number of processes.
captured_features: differentiation into astrocytes, oligodendrocytes, and neurons
summaries.population: Primary striatal neurons and embryonic neural stem cells

summaries.title: Repeated holdout validation for weighted quantile sum regression.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Data from the Swedish Environmental Longitudinal, Mother and Child, Asthma and Allergy (SELMA) study.
summaries.summary: The study proposes and validates a 'repeated holdout validation' method for Weighted Quantile Sum (WQS) regression to assess chemical mixtures in small environmental epidemiology samples, using prenatal exposure to 26 endocrine disrupting chemicals and child IQ.
key_findings: Repeated holdout validation improves the stability of WQS estimates in finite study samples.
WQS index estimate was significant without partitioning but attenuated and became nonsignificant using repeated holdout validation.
Repeated holdout can characterize uncertainty in identifying chemicals of concern.
captured_features: prenatal exposure to 26 endocrine disrupting chemicals
summaries.study_design: Methodological validation using a cohort study
summaries.population: 718 mother-child pairs

summaries.title: Early-life inhalant allergen exposure, filaggrin genotype, and the development of sensitization from infancy to adolescence.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses a population-based birth cohort and home dust samples, not electronic health records.
summaries.summary: A longitudinal birth cohort study investigating how filaggrin (FLG) mutations modify the relationship between early-life inhalant allergen exposure (mite, cat, dog) and the development of allergic sensitization from infancy to adolescence.
key_findings: Significant interaction between FLG mutations and Fel d 1 exposure on cat sensitization, with higher risk in children with mutations.
Mite sensitization risk with Der p 1 exposure was consistently greater in children with FLG mutations.
Significant interaction between FLG mutations and dog ownership, where children with mutations exposed to dogs in infancy had significantly lower risk of sensitization.
captured_features: Mite, cat, and dog allergen levels in home dust
summaries.population: 1184 subjects in a population-based birth cohort (infancy to age 16 years)
summaries.exposure_domain: Inhalant allergens (house dust mite, cat, dog)
limitations: Not specified in the provided text

summaries.title: Childhood type 1 diabetes: an environment-wide association study across England.
summaries.ehr_evidence: We obtained type 1 diabetes data from National Health Service (NHS) Digital hospital episode statistics (HES) records
summaries.summary: An ecological environment-wide association study (EnW AS) assessing associations between various environmental, demographic, and meteorological factors and childhood type 1 diabetes incidence in England.
key_findings: 15 out of 53 demographic and environmental risk factors were significantly associated with diabetes incidence
Air pollutants (PM10, NO2, NOx, CO) were inversely associated with incidence
Significant associations found with lead in soil, radon, outdoor light at night, overcrowding, population density, and ethnicity
Association found between type 1 diabetes and Index of Multiple Deprivation (IMD) and radon potential class
summaries.population: children aged 0–9 years in England
summaries.exposure_domain: air pollution, soil lead, radon, outdoor light at night, demographics, meteorological factors

summaries.title: Unilateral morning glory disc anomaly in a patient with prenatal Zika virus exposure.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study is a single case report.
summaries.summary: A case report describing a 3-year-old male child with morning glory disc anomaly (MGDA) and other neurological defects following prenatal Zika virus (ZIKV) exposure.
key_findings: First report suggesting an association between prenatal ZIKV exposure and MGDA.
Prenatal ZIKV exposure may be associated with a wider pathologic spectrum than previously reported.
captured_features: Neuroimaging (base of skull encephalocele, callosal dysgenesis)
summaries.population: 3-year-old male child

summaries.title: Early-Life Famine Exposure and Risk of Cardiovascular Diseases in Later Life: Findings From the REACTION Study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used data from the REACTION (Risk Evaluation of Cancers in Chinese Diabetic Individuals: A Longitudinal) Study, which recruited community-dwelling adults.
summaries.summary: The study investigated the association between early-life exposure to the Chinese famine (1959-1962) and the risk of cardiovascular diseases (CVD) in adulthood.
key_findings: Early-life famine exposure (fetal, childhood, and adolescent) significantly increased the risk of total CVD, myocardial infarction, stroke, and coronary heart disease.
Individuals in severely affected famine areas had a significantly increased risk of total CVD in all three exposed groups.
summaries.population: 259,657 community-dwelling adults aged 40 years or older from mainland China

summaries.title: Long-Term Effects of Environmental Lead on Erythropoietin Production in Young Adults: A Follow-Up Study of a Prospective Cohort in Kosovo.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses a prospective birth cohort and a biorepository for blood samples, not EHR or linked health data.
summaries.summary: A follow-up study of a prospective birth cohort in Kosovo to evaluate the long-term effects of prenatal, early childhood, and concurrent lead (Pb) exposure on erythropoietin (EPO) production in young adulthood.
key_findings: Participants exposed to high Pb levels required increased EPO production to maintain normal hemoglobin (Hgb) at ages 4.5 and 6.5.
By ages 9.5 and 12, participants were no longer capable of hyper-production of EPO, suggesting cumulative toxicity to renal peritubular cells.
Reduction in Pb exposure may allow for a reversal of the impact on EPO production.
summaries.population: 101 participants (mean age 24.9 years) from a prospective birth cohort in Kosovo
limitations: Challenges in recruiting participants from Prishtina due to city expansion and demographic changes.

summaries.title: DNA methylome signatures of prenatal exposure to synthetic glucocorticoids in hippocampus and peripheral whole blood of female guinea pigs in early life.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study uses a guinea pig animal model.
summaries.summary: The study examines DNA methylation signatures in the hippocampus and whole blood of guinea pig offspring following prenatal exposure to synthetic glucocorticoids (sGC) to assess epigenetic biomarkers of common molecular pathways.
key_findings: Identified tissue-specific and common methylation signatures of prenatal sGC exposure.
134 genes were modified in both hippocampus and blood, with 61 showing changes at identical CpG sites.
Prenatal sGC exposure alters the methylation status of gene clusters involved in brain development.
summaries.population: Female guinea pig offspring

summaries.title: Assessment of chronic bronchitis and risk factors in young adults: results from BAMSE.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used questionnaire data and clinical measures from the Swedish BAMSE birth cohort, not electronic health records.
summaries.summary: Assessment of the prevalence and early-life risk factors (e.g., air pollution, breastfeeding) for chronic bronchitis in young adults using the Swedish BAMSE birth cohort.
key_findings: Prevalence of chronic bronchitis in young adults was 5.5%.
Daily smoking (aOR 3.85) and air pollution (black carbon at ages 1-4y, aOR 1.71) were associated with chronic bronchitis.
Exclusive breastfeeding for $\ge$4 months was associated with a lower risk of chronic bronchitis (aOR 0.66).
summaries.population: Young adults in a Swedish population-based birth cohort
limitations: Not specified in the provided text

summaries.title: Aspartame and cancer - new evidence for causation.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None. The study is a toxicological analysis of rodent models.
summaries.summary: A commentary and re-evaluation of Ramazzini Institute studies confirming that aspartame is a chemical carcinogen in rodents, with significant risks from prenatal exposure.
key_findings: Aspartame causes dose-related increases in malignant tumors in rodents.
Prenatal exposure to aspartame increases cancer risk in rodent offspring.
Immunohistochemical and morphological re-evaluation confirmed malignancy in 92.3% of cases.
summaries.population: Sprague-Dawley rats and Swiss mice

summaries.title: Maternal high-fat diet modifies myelin organization, microglial interactions, and results in social memory and sensorimotor gating deficits in adolescent mouse offspring.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None. The study uses a mouse model.
summaries.summary: Investigation of how maternal high-fat diet (mHFD) affects myelin organization, microglial interactions, and behavioral outcomes in adolescent mouse offspring.
key_findings: mHFD results in neuroinflammation and hypomyelination
increased myelin density with reduced cytosolic myelin channels in the corpus callosum of male offspring
lower transcripts of myelination-associated genes (e.g., Igf1) in the hippocampus of male offspring
microglia in the corpus callosum showed reduced mature lysosomes and increased synaptic contacts
mHFD-exposed offspring exhibited deficits in social memory and sensorimotor gating
summaries.population: Adolescent mouse offspring

summaries.title: The association between in utero exposure to maternal psychological stress and female reproductive function in adolescence: A prospective cohort study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study is a population-based pregnancy cohort study (The Raine Study) using prospective reporting and clinical examinations, not EHR.
summaries.summary: A prospective cohort study investigating the association between maternal psychological stress during pregnancy and reproductive function in female offspring during adolescence.
key_findings: Maternal stress in late gestation was associated with greater uterine volume and higher ovarian antral follicle count (AFC) in adolescents.
No associations were found between maternal stress and circulating AMH or Inhibin B.
captured_features: maternal stressful life events (early and late gestation)
summaries.population: 228 female offspring (age 14-16 years)

summaries.title: Early life exposure to house dust mite allergen prevents experimental allergic asthma requiring mitochondrial H(2)O(2).
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study is an experimental animal model using mice.
summaries.summary: The study investigates the role of mitochondrial H2O2 in establishing airway tolerance to house dust mite (HDM) allergens in mice, demonstrating that early-life low-dose exposure prevents later allergic airway inflammation.
key_findings: Early-life exposure to low-dose HDM prevents allergic airway inflammation in later life.
Depletion of mitochondrial H2O2 in MCAT mice abrogated HDM-induced airway tolerance.
Low-dose HDM exposure constrains lung dendritic cell numbers to establish tolerance.
summaries.population: Newborn and young mice

summaries.title: Characterisation of lung function trajectories and associated early-life predictors in an Australian birth cohort study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The Raine Study is a large prospective cohort of 2900 pregnant women recruited from King Edward Memorial Hospital... Clinical and demographic data were collected during pregnancy and offspring assessed before birth and at 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 10, 14, 17, 18, 20, 22, 27 and 28 years of age.
summaries.summary: Characterization of lung function trajectories (FEV1, FVC, FEV1/FVC) from 6 to 22 years and identification of early-life predictors in an Australian birth cohort.
key_findings: Four lung function trajectories identified
Asthma and maternal smoking identified as risk factors for low lung function trajectories
Early-life exposure to PM2.5 associated with low lung function trajectories
summaries.population: 1512 participants (children/young adults) from the Raine Study, Australia

summaries.title: Prenatal exposure to insecticides and child cardiometabolic risk factors in the VHEMBE birth cohort.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses a birth cohort (VHEMBE) and peripartum biological samples, not EHR.
summaries.summary: Study investigating the impact of prenatal exposure to DDT and pyrethroid insecticides on cardiometabolic health markers in children at 5 years of age in South Africa.
key_findings: Maternal pyrethroid metabolites were associated with lower adiposity (reduced BMI z-scores, smaller waist circumferences, and decreased body fat percentages).
Maternal p,p’-DDT concentrations were associated with a reduction in body fat percentage.
summaries.population: Children in Limpopo, South Africa

summaries.title: Early-life exposure to hardship increased risk tolerance and entrepreneurship in adulthood with gender differences.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study uses geographical differences in famine intensity and weather shocks.
summaries.summary: The study investigates how early-life hardship during China's Great Famine (1959-1961) affected adult risk tolerance and entrepreneurship, specifically looking at gender differences and the mechanism of conditioning.
key_findings: Hardship during formative years increased risk tolerance in both men and women.
Hardship increased business ownership only among men.
The increase in entrepreneurship was at least partly due to conditioning rather than selection.
summaries.population: Chinese population (Great Famine cohort)
limitations: Specific to the historical context of China's Great Famine

summaries.title: Repeatable enhancement of healthcare data with social determinants of health.
summaries.ehr_evidence: linking compiled consumer data to patient records in Electronic Health Records
summaries.summary: Development of a repeatable process to enrich Electronic Health Records (EHR) with social determinants of health (SDOH) data from commercial consumer sources.
key_findings: SDOH factors like home-ownership, marital-status, presence of children, number of members per household, economic stability, and education were significantly different between COVID-19 positive and negative groups.
Estimated family-income and home market-value were not significantly different.
summaries.population: patients (COVID-19 positive and negative groups)
limitations: consumer data coverage ranged from 90% to 54%

summaries.title: Maternal use of methamphetamine induces sex-dependent changes in myocardial gene expression in adult offspring.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: Investigation into how prenatal methamphetamine exposure affects myocardial gene expression in adult offspring, showing sex-dependent changes.
key_findings: Prenatal methamphetamine exposure induces sex-dependent changes in myocardial gene expression in adult offspring.
Female offspring exhibited 184 gene expression changes compared to 74 in males.
Dimethylarginine dimethylaminohydrolase 2 and 3-hydroxybutyrate dehydrogenase 1 were under-expressed in females.
summaries.population: Prenatally exposed rat offspring

summaries.title: Quantile regression to examine the association of air pollution with subclinical atherosclerosis in an adolescent population.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Dutch PIAMA birth cohort
summaries.summary: Cross-sectional study using quantile regression to examine the association between air pollution (NO2, PM2.5, PM2.5 absorbance, PMcoarse, and ultrafine particles) and carotid intima-media thickness (CIMT) in adolescents.
key_findings: Not provided in the text snippet
summaries.population: 363 adolescents (age 16 years)
limitations: Results not provided in the snippet

summaries.title: Cognitive and Behavioral Development of 9-Year-Old Children After Maternal Cancer During Pregnancy: A Prospective Multicenter Cohort Study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used a neurocognitive test battery and parent-report behavioral questionnaires rather than EHR.
summaries.summary: A multicenter cohort study evaluating the long-term cognitive and behavioral outcomes of 9-year-old children prenatally exposed to maternal cancer and its treatment.
key_findings: Mean cognitive and behavioral outcomes were within normal ranges.
Gestational age at birth showed a positive association with Full Scale Intelligence Quotient (FSIQ).
No difference in FSIQ was found between treatment types.
Associations were observed with preterm birth, maternal death, and maternal education.
summaries.population: 151 children (mean age 9.3 years) prenatally exposed to maternal cancer
summaries.exposure_domain: maternal cancer and treatment during pregnancy

summaries.title: Evaluating Demographic Data to Improve Confidence in Equity Analytics in a Children's Hospital.
summaries.ehr_evidence: the electronic health record team standardized race and ethnicity fields... all fields are sourced from the same electronic health record field.
summaries.summary: Evaluation of demographic data collection, analysis, and visualization infrastructure to improve health equity analytics at Akron Children’s Hospital.
key_findings: Fields were not consistently accessible across reporting tools.
All fields were sourced from the same electronic health record field when present.
Identified inconsistencies in demographic data visualization.
summaries.population: Pediatric patients at Akron Children’s Hospital
summaries.exposure_domain: Demographic data and social determinants of health (SDOH)
limitations: limitations to a seldom-populated Race 2 field

summaries.title: The exposome in respiratory diseases: multiple preventable risk factors from early life to adulthood.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The studies reviewed use the INMA, RHINESSA, and NutriNet-Santé cohorts, which are population-based cohorts.
summaries.summary: A journal club review of three studies investigating the impact of environmental exposures (phthalates, air pollution, and exposome profiles) on respiratory health outcomes from early life to adulthood.
key_findings: Regulation and reduction of phthalates and air pollution are supported.
Risk reduction requires a multi-interventional approach targeting early-life factors and healthy lifestyles in adulthood.
summaries.population: Children and adults
limitations: Exposome research in respiratory diseases is still in its infancy

summaries.title: Parental, gestational, and early-life exposure to indoor environmental hazardous factors on allergic rhinitis among preschool children in Urumqi City: a case-control study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used parental reports and a case-control survey design rather than electronic health records.
summaries.summary: A case-control study investigating the association between early-life indoor environmental exposures (maternal exposure during pregnancy and child's first year) and allergic rhinitis in preschool children in Urumqi City, China.
key_findings: Cesarean section (OR = 1.31)
Father with AR (OR = 2.67)
Mother with AR (OR = 3.70)
Mother with asthma (OR = 3.11)
Mother with newly purchased furniture during pregnancy (OR = 1.49)
captured_features: Indoor environmental correlates of maternal exposure
summaries.population: Preschool children in Urumqi City, China

summaries.title: Associations of longitudinal BMI percentile classification patterns in early childhood with neighborhood-level social determinants of health.
summaries.ehr_evidence: We extracted EHR data from 2012-2019 for a children’s health system that includes 2 hospitals and wide network of outpatient clinics spanning 5 East Coast states in the US.
summaries.summary: Longitudinal analysis of BMI-percentile patterns in children aged 0-7 years and their association with neighborhood-level social determinants of health (SDOH) using EHR data.
key_findings: Five BMI-percentile classification groups identified: always obesity, overweight most of the time, increasing, decreasing, and always normal weight.
Children in the first three groups were more likely to live in neighborhoods with higher poverty, unemployment, crowded households, single-parent households, and lower preschool enrollment.
summaries.study_design: longitudinal clustering and multinomial logistic regression
summaries.population: 36,910 children aged 0-7 years in 5 East Coast states, US
limitations: Not specified in the provided text

summaries.title: Correction: Neonatal blood lead concentration predicts medium term lead-related outcomes in children ≤5 years old with congenital lead poisoning: A retrospective cohort study in Northern Nigeria.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None mentioned in the correction notice.
summaries.summary: Correction notice for a retrospective cohort study on neonatal blood lead concentration and medium-term outcomes in children <5 years old in Northern Nigeria.
summaries.population: children <5 years old with congenital lead poisoning in Northern Nigeria

summaries.title: Association between prenatal exposure to alkylphenols and intelligence quotient among preschool children: sex-specific effects.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used the Guangxi Zhuang Birth Cohort, which is a prospective birth cohort, not an EHR or linked health data source.
summaries.summary: The study investigated the association between prenatal exposure to alkylphenols (APs) and the intelligence quotient (IQ) of preschool children, exploring sex-specific and nonmonotonic effects.
key_findings: Prenatal exposure to NP and 4-T-OP (second tertile) showed an inverse association with full-scale IQ (FSIQ) and working memory index (WMI).
Prenatal exposure to 4-N-NP (third tertile) showed a positive association with the fluid reasoning index in total children and girls.
A U-shaped relationship was observed between maternal 4-T-OP and WMI in total children and girls.
Combined AP mixtures above the 50th percentile exhibited an inverse trend on FSIQ in total children and girls.
captured_features: Maternal serum concentrations of NP, 4-T-OP, 4-N-NP, and 4-n-octylphenol
Childhood IQ scores (FSIQ, WMI, fluid reasoning index)
summaries.population: 221 mother-child pairs from the Guangxi Zhuang Birth Cohort

summaries.title: Activated fibroblasts modify keratinocyte stem niche through TET1 and IL-6 to promote their rapid transformation in a mouse model of prenatal arsenic exposure.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: This study investigates the mechanism by which prenatal arsenic exposure promotes skin tumorigenesis in mouse offspring by activating dermal fibroblasts to create a pro-tumorigenic niche for keratinocyte stem cells.
key_findings: Prenatal arsenic exposure induces activation markers (α-SMA, Fibronectin, Collagen IV) and TET1 in dermal fibroblasts.
Activated fibroblasts secrete high levels of IL-6, driven by TET1-mediated epigenetic changes.
IL-6 promotes proliferation, migration, and survival of keratinocyte stem cells via JAK2-STAT3 signaling.
IL-6 induces epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition (EMT) in keratinocyte stem cells, facilitating skin tumor development.
summaries.population: BALB/c mouse offspring

summaries.title: Association between prenatal glucocorticoid exposure and adolescent neurodevelopment: An observational follow-up study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study is a prospective observational follow-up study using clinical measurements rather than EHR or linked health data.
summaries.summary: A prospective observational follow-up study investigating the long-term effects of prenatal betamethasone exposure on neurodevelopmental outcomes in adolescents.
key_findings: No significant differences in global IQ, ADHD symptoms, metabolic markers, HPAA, or ANS activity.
Reduced electrocortical activity in the frontal brain region in the betamethasone group.
summaries.population: 21 adolescents (14-15 years old) exposed to prenatal betamethasone and 35 reference children

summaries.title: Newborn glomerular function and gestational particulate air pollution.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses the ENVIRONAGE cohort and a Dutch questionnaire rather than EHR data.
summaries.summary: The study investigates the impact of gestational particulate air pollution (black carbon and PM2.5) on newborn glomerular function, using cord blood cystatin C as a biomarker.
key_findings: Gestational exposure to black carbon (BC) and fine particulate matter (PM2.5) was associated with increased cord blood cystatin C levels (p < 0.01).
The strongest associations were observed from the third trimester onwards.
summaries.population: 1484 mother-child pairs in Genk, Belgium

summaries.title: Associations between gestational exposure to perfluoroalkyl substances, fetal growth, and the mediation effect of thyroid hormones.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used data from the Ezhou birth cohort study, which is a cohort study, not an EHR.
summaries.summary: The study investigated the association between maternal serum perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFASs) and neonatal growth metrics, specifically examining the mediating role of maternal thyroid hormones.
key_findings: PFNA and PFHxS were negatively associated with birth length.
PFOA and PFHxS were negatively associated with birth head circumference.
Maternal free triiodothyronine (FT3) mediated 36.7% of the negative association between PFNA and birth length.
Maternal free thyroxine (FT4) mediated 30.8% of the effect of PFOA on head circumference.
captured_features: Maternal thyroid hormone levels (FT3, FT4, TSH, TT4, TT3, TG)
summaries.population: 374 mother-infant pairs from the Ezhou birth cohort study (2019-2020)

summaries.title: Early Life Exposure to Manure-Fertilized Soil Shapes the Gut Antibiotic Resistome.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None. The study is an experimental animal study using mice.
summaries.summary: Experimental study investigating how early life exposure to manure-fertilized soil shapes the gut antibiotic resistome in mice.
key_findings: Manure fertilization enriched tetracycline-resistant ARGs in both red and black soils.
Mice in these environments harbored markedly higher abundances of ARGs, particularly the tet(Q) gene.
Muribaculaceae and Bacteroidaceae were identified as key hosts of tet(Q).
summaries.population: Mice

summaries.title: A comprehensive atlas of the bonobo gut bacteriome and its associated host and exposome factors.
summaries.ehr_evidence: No EHR used; data were collected from 644 fecal samples and direct host/exposome measurements from wild and zoo-housed bonobos.
summaries.summary: A comprehensive atlas of the bonobo gut bacteriome and its associated host and exposome factors, comparing wild and zoo-housed populations to understand the effects of industrialization and social contact.
key_findings: Clear differences in gut bacterial diversity and composition between wild and captive bonobos
Social contact is a stronger predictor of bacteriome similarity than genetics or other exposome factors
Maternal effects persist even in non-cohabiting mother-offspring dyads
summaries.data_source_type: Primary data collection (fecal samples and host/exposome data)
summaries.population: 212 wild and zoo-housed bonobos (Pan paniscus)
summaries.exposure_domain: Exposome (diet, transit time, early life adversity, medication use, social contact, maternal effects)

summaries.title: Using machine learning to predict future foster care admission.
summaries.ehr_evidence: We use claims data for children enrolled in a Medicaid health plan in Ohio as well as for linked adults, along with data on individual and geographic social determinants of health (SDOH) factors.
summaries.summary: The study evaluates the use of machine learning (gradient-boosted trees) to predict future foster care admissions in children by utilizing Medicaid claims data and social determinants of health (SDOH) factors.
key_findings: The gradient-boosted tree model identified 2408 children (1.32%) at risk of foster care admission.
The model outperformed logistic regression.
The inclusion of SDOH data significantly improved predictive accuracy, increasing the positive predictive value (PPV) from 27.44% to 84.72%.
captured_features: individual and geographic social determinants of health (SDOH) factors
summaries.population: Children enrolled in a Medicaid health plan in Ohio
limitations: Ethical and legal concerns regarding the use of such tools for selecting families for child welfare investigations
SDOH data was only available for 85% of the children

summaries.title: Moderate prenatal alcohol exposure differentially alters acute ethanol sensitivity of GABAergic transmission in CRFR1- and CRFR1+ CeM Neurons.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: Laboratory study investigating how moderate prenatal alcohol exposure (PAE) alters acute ethanol's modulation of GABAergic neurotransmission in the central amygdala (CeM) of adult rats.
key_findings: PAE alters acute EtOH modulation of GABA transmission in a cell-type and concentration-dependent manner.
Effects are sex-specific in adult offspring.
summaries.population: Adult male and female rats (P80-105)

summaries.title: Sugar restriction in the first 1000 days after conception, and long-term respiratory health: a quasi-experiment study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Outcomes included incident asthma, COPD, and IPF (ascertained by health records)
summaries.summary: A quasi-experimental study investigating the long-term impact of early-life sugar restriction (based on the 1953 UK sugar rationing) on adult respiratory health and lung function using UK Biobank data.
key_findings: Early-life sugar rationing associated with lower risks of asthma (HR 0.75) and COPD (HR 0.73).
Delayed disease onset by ≤ 3.6 years.
Rationed participants showed higher mean FEV1%, FVC%, and FEV1/FVC ratio.
Diabetes and hypertension mediated 18.2% of the effect.
captured_features: demographic, socioeconomic, lifestyle, and clinical factors
summaries.population: 58,670 UK Biobank participants born 1951–1956

summaries.title: Impact of maternal fasting blood glucose in pregnancy on body composition, anthropometric, and metabolic outcomes in newborns and 3-Year-Old offspring: results from the lifestyle in pregnancy and offspring study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The analysis used data from the Lifestyle in Pregnancy (LiP) study and its 3-year follow-up, the LiPO study.
summaries.summary: Study investigating whether maternal fasting glucose predicts offspring body composition, anthropometry, and metabolic outcomes at birth and at 3 years.
key_findings: Higher maternal fasting glucose was associated with a higher birth weight z-score.
Glucose ≥ 5.6 mmol/L predicted higher birth weight.
No significant associations were found for BMI z-score or metabolic markers at 3 years.
summaries.population: 301 women in LiP study, 157 in LiPO study, 75 children with DXA assessments
limitations: Small sample size for DXA assessments (n=75)
